topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving, regulating, and ensuring access to Medicare and Medicaid services.

Document 1: Amends titles XVIII (Medicare) and XIX (Medicaid) to cover a Voluntary Advance Care Planning Consultation.
Requires that, in the absence of a validly executed advance directive, any authentic expression of a person's wishes with respect to health care be honored.
Document 2: Increases criminal penalties for both felony and misdemeanor fraud under SSA titles XVIII (Medicare) and XIX (Medicaid).
Extends the statute of limitations from six to 10 years after presentation of a claim.
Document 3: Prohibits the Secretary of Health and Human Services from applying a multiple procedure payment reduction policy to the professional component of imaging services.

Document 4: Amends part B of title XVIII (Medicare) with respect to certain factors and indices in geographic adjustments to the fee schedule.

Document 5: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) to place limitations on mandatory overtime for nurses.
Establish civil money penalties for violations of this Act.
Document 6: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) to require state Medicaid plans to give an individual with disabilities the choice to receive care in a home and community-based setting.
Prescribes requirements for providing in home and community-based settings those services.
Document 7: Revises the factors that the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services must consider with respect to determining compliance with Medicare program requirements.

increased border-related cooperation with Mexico

topic.name: EMP Weapons Accountability
topic.description: Legislation focused on assessing and reporting the development and deployment of electromagnetic pulse weapons by foreign nations.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.
Document 2: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.

topic.name: Wilderness and Land Management Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts focused on the designation, protection, and management of wilderness areas and public lands in the United States.

redesignate the Olympic Wilderness as the 'Daniel J. Evans Wilderness'.
Document 2: directs the Secretary of Agriculture to convey to the city of Fruit Heights

Document 3: designates specified Bureau of Land Management and National Forest System lands in California as wilderness areas
designates certain land in Los Padres National Forest as the Machesna Mountain Potential Wilderness Area.

requires approval of all parties for any such conveyance.

topic.name: Child Welfare and Protection Laws
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislation focused on the welfare, protection, and rights of children, particularly in the context of foster care, immigration status, and victim protections.

Document 7: Help Separated Families Act of 2013 - ensures immigration status does not disqualify relatives from child placement.

topic.name: Mental Health Services Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving mental health care services and support systems for children, adolescents, and the general population.

Document 1: authorize the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish programs related to mental health services for children and adolescents
awarding scholarships to students who agree to work as child and adolescent mental health service professionals
Document 2: establish the Commission for Evidence-Based Mental and Substance Use Health Care
change Medicaid reimbursement rules to cover same-day visits for primary care services and mental health and substance use services
Document 3: establish a National Mental Health Policy Laboratory
evaluate and disseminate evidence-based practices and services delivery models
Document 4: authorize treatment of autism spectrum disorders
Document 5: expand the definition of 'health care provider' for purposes of health information technology provisions
include qualified clinical psychologists, clinical social workers, psychiatric hospitals in the health professionals eligible for incentive payments

focus primarily on the early detection and intervention of severe mental illness in young people
Document 7: include as a health care provider behavioral and mental health professionals
makes inpatient psychiatric hospitals eligible for payment for inpatient hospital services

topic.name: Prescription Drug Regulation
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative acts aimed at regulating prescription drugs, cosmetics, and medical gases to ensure safety, affordability, and proper labeling.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA) to repeal provisions restricting the importation of prescription drugs.
Prohibits the Secretary from taking any action against any of the persons involved with the interstate sale of a prescription drug.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require a liquid formulation of an over-the-counter drug to be packaged with a dosage delivery device.
Defines 'dosage delivery device' as an object that is designed to measure the dosage of a drug in liquid form.
Document 3: This bill amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require cosmetics companies to register their facilities with the Food and Drug Administration (FDA).
The FDA must review the safety of at least five cosmetic ingredients each year.
Document 4: Amends part D (Voluntary Prescription Drug Benefit Program) of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act (SSAct) to require drug manufacturers to pay the Secretary of Health and Human Services drug rebates.
Excludes from Medicare coverage as a part D drug any drug or biological manufactured by a manufacturer that has not entered into and have in effect a rebate agreement.
Document 5: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to set forth provisions regarding the regulation of medical gases.
Requires the Secretary to establish regulations for medical gases, including appropriate current good manufacturing practice requirements.
Document 6: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to expand electronic logbook requirements applicable to sellers and purchasers of listed chemicals.
Establishes a preference for grant recipients who develop electronic logbook systems that are statewide.
Document 7: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act (SSA) with respect to prescription drug plans to repeal the prohibition against interference by the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS).
Grants the Secretary authority similar to that of other federal entities that purchase prescription drugs in bulk to negotiate contracts with manufacturers.

topic.name: Ecosystem Restoration and Water Management
topic.description: The documents primarily focus on legislative acts aimed at ecosystem restoration, water quality improvement, and water resource management across various regions in the United States.

Document 5: project for ecosystem restoration, water supply, flood control

topic.name: Mortgage Regulation and Protection
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at regulating mortgage practices, protecting homeowners from fraud, and facilitating homeownership for vulnerable populations.

Document 1: Amends the National Housing Act of 1937 to modify the eligibility criteria for insurance of home equity conversion mortgages (reverse mortgages) of elderly home owners.
Directs the Secretary to promulgate regulations to protect elderly homeowners from the marketing of financial and insurance products not in their interest.
Document 2: Amends the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act of 2008 to terminate the authority of the Secretary of the Treasury to provide new mortgage modification assistance under the Home Affordable Modification Program (HAMP).
Document 3: Establish in the Federal Housing Administration (FHA) a Second Chance at Homeownership Program to create a voluntary FHA program to provide lenders for qualified second-chance borrowers.
Target mortgage assistance to enable families to become homeowners of a one- to four-family principal residence.
Document 4: Requires the Director of the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) to assign the Chief of its Financial Crimes Section to be the Nationwide Mortgage Fraud Coordinator.
Establish regional task forces to organize initiatives to address mortgage fraud.
Document 5: Amends the S.A.F.E. Mortgage Licensing Act of 2008 to exclude a seller of manufactured homes from the definition of loan originator.
Amends the Truth in Lending Act to revise the definition of 'high cost mortgage.'
Document 6: Prohibits a foreclosure consultant from receiving compensation from a homeowner for services performed regarding residential real property until such consultant has fully performed each service contracted for.
Empowers the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) and the states to enforce this Act.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to make grants to states for providing foreclosure-mitigation counseling to homeowners of owner-occupied single family homes with mortgage loans in default.
Requires each state recipient to provide at least 95% of the grant amounts to nonprofit entities.

topic.name: Coral Reef Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various amendments and acts aimed at the conservation and restoration of coral reef ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to direct the Secretary of Commerce to make grants for projects for the conservation of coral reef ecosystems.
Authorizes the Secretary to conduct activities, including with local, regional, or international programs and partners, to conserve coral reef ecosystems.
Document 2: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects.
Authorizes actions to minimize injury to a coral reef or loss of an ecosystem function resulting from human activities.
Document 3: This bill amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act to authorize the 12 federal agencies on the U.S. Coral Reef Task Force to carry out, either individually or cooperatively, prize competitions that promote coral reef research and conservation.
Priority is given to establish programs that address communities, environments, or industries that are in distress due to the decline or degradation of coral reef ecosystems.
Document 4: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to transfer certain authority from the Administrator of NOAA to the Secretary of Commerce.
Directs the Secretary to give priority to community-based local action strategies when awarding certain grants for conservation projects.
Document 5: This bill amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects, including monitoring and assessment, research, pollution reduction, education, and technical support.
NOAA must also submit an international coral reef ecosystem strategy to Congress and may establish an international coral reef ecosystem partnership program.
Document 6: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to direct the Secretary of Commerce to submit to specified congressional committees a national coral reef ecosystem action strategy.
Authorizes the Secretary to make agreements with nonprofit organizations for public-private partnerships promoting coral reef ecosystem conservation.
Document 7: This bill amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to expand the coral reef conservation program by awarding remaining program grant funds to appropriate projects.
The U.S. Coral Reef Task Force is authorized to carry out prize competitions that promote coral reef research and conservation.

topic.name: Agricultural Policy and Support Programs
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at supporting farmers, enhancing agricultural programs, and improving food assistance initiatives.

Document 1: This bill amends various agricultural laws to modify and establish programs to assist beginning farmers.
The bill modifies several agricultural programs to require the Farm Service Agency (FSA) to prequalify loan applicants.
Document 2: Amends the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act to allow schools and local educational agencies (LEAs) to receive special assistance payments for free or reduced price meals.
Requires schools and LEAs reimbursed on the basis of estimates or certifications to provide free meals to all their students.
Document 3: Amends the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009 to extend the benefit calculation of 113.6 % of the June 2008 value of the thrifty food plan for the supplemental nutrition assistance program (SNAP).
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA), upon the petition and approval of California dairy producers, to designate California as a separate federal milk marketing order.
Document 5: Amends part B (Rural Education Initiative) of title VI of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to revise the Small, Rural School Achievement program.
Limits eligibility to LEAs whose schools are all designated with a school locale code of Fringe Rural, Distant Rural, or Remote Rural.
Document 6: Amends the Agricultural Adjustment Act to direct the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to base the minimum Class II milk price on the national average cost of production.
Directs the Secretary to identify all dairy products imported into and exported from the United States.
Document 7: Amends the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008 to consider mung beans and pulse crops as covered commodities.
Requires producers to demonstrate that they have entered into a contract to produce a fruit or vegetable crop for processing.

topic.name: Energy Policy and Regulation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to energy production, renewable resources, and regulatory frameworks governing energy markets.

Document 1: Amends the Commodity Exchange Act to direct the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) to treat a utility operations-related swap entered into with a utility special entity as if such swap were entered into with an entity that is not a special entity.
Defines "utility operations-related swap" as one that is entered into to hedge or mitigate commercial risk.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to repeal various tax credits related to alternative fuels and renewable energy sources.
Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to prescribe a flat income tax rate for corporations based on the overall revenue savings from the repeal of energy tax expenditures.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a new tax credit for investment in projects for manufacturing alternative energy equipment.
Extends the tax credit for producing electricity from renewable resources and expands such credit to include the production of thermal energy and zero carbon emissions resource facilities.
Document 4: Amends the Clean Air Act to prescribe the volume of renewable fuel that gasoline sold in the United States must contain in calendar years 2013 through 2025.
Provides that 1.0 gallon of a fuel blend containing 85% ethanol and 15% gasoline shall be considered to be the equivalent of 1.5 gallons of renewable fuel.
Document 5: Amends the Public Works and Economic Development Act of 1965 to revise the economic adjustment assistance grant program.
Directs the Secretary of Labor to award national emergency grants to an eligible entity to provide employment and training assistance to manufacturing workers.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Energy to establish a competitive research, development, demonstration, and commercial application program for advanced heavy duty hybrid vehicles.
Requires each grant recipient to complete research and demonstration of advanced hybrid technology.
Document 7: Requires the Secretary of Energy to establish a program to deploy and integrate plug-in electric drive vehicles in multiple regions.
Lists program goals to demonstrate the viability of a vehicle-based transportation system that is not overly dependent on petroleum.

topic.name: Data Privacy and Security Legislation
topic.description: A collection of laws focused on the protection of personally identifiable information and the requirements for notifying individuals in the event of a data breach.

Document 1: Requires any federal agency or business entity engaged in interstate commerce that uses, accesses, transmits, stores, disposes of, or collects sensitive personally identifiable information, following the discovery of a security breach, to notify any U.S. resident whose information has been accessed or acquired.
Allows a delay of notification for law enforcement and national security purposes.
Document 2: Requires any agency or business entity with sensitive personally identifiable information to notify without unreasonable delay any U.S. resident of a security breach.

Exempts agencies or business entities from security breach notification requirements if they provide written certification to the Secret Service that providing such notification would impede a criminal investigation or damage national security.
Document 3: Makes it unlawful for any officer or employee of the federal government to willfully make an unauthorized disclosure of personally identifiable covered information.
Defines 'personally identifiable covered information' as return, return information, and taxpayer return information unless the disclosure of such information is authorized by law.
Document 4: Requires the Department of Health and Human Services to notify an individual within two business days after discovery of any breach of security of any system maintained by a health care exchange established under the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act.
... which is known to have resulted in the theft or unlawful access of the individual's personally identifiable information.
Document 5: Directs the General Services Administration to procure an antivirus product suitable for federal agencies to provide to individuals whose personally identifiable information may have been compromised.

... a federal agency that experiences a compromise in its computer systems resulting in the loss of personally identifiable information to provide to any individual directly affected a one-year license of such product.
Document 6: Makes it unlawful for any officer or employee of the federal government to willfully make an unauthorized disclosure of personally identifiable covered information.

Defines 'personally identifiable covered information' as any information contained in a financial record if such information includes the name, social security number, street address, telephone number, or electronic mail address of, or other personally identifiable information concerning, a consumer.
Document 7: Defines 'sensitive personally identifiable information' to include specified combinations of data elements in electronic or digital form.
Imposes a prison term of up to five years and/or a fine on any individual who has knowledge of and intentionally and willfully conceals a security breach.

topic.name: Tax Fraud and Identity Theft Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at preventing tax fraud and identity theft through amendments to the Internal Revenue Code and the establishment of regulatory frameworks.

Document 1: amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to the disclosure of tax return information in cases of identity theft
imposes new criminal and civil penalties for tax-related identity theft
Document 2: amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to the disclosure of tax return information in cases of identity theft
imposes new criminal and civil penalties for tax-related identity theft
Document 6: direct the Department of the Treasury to establish a program to license or certify and regulate tax return preparers
require tax return preparers to provide a disclosure statement to a consumer about the arrangement
Document 7: establish a plan to reduce the administrative time required to process and resolve cases of tax-related identity theft
ensure that taxpayers who have been adversely affected by identity theft have a single point of contact at the Internal Revenue Service (IRS)

topic.name: Banking Regulation and Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts aimed at regulating banking practices, enhancing financial stability, and addressing the relationships between depository institutions and securities firms.

Document 1: Prohibits an insured depository institution from being an affiliate of any broker or dealer, investment adviser, investment company, or any other person or entity engaged principally in the issue, flotation, underwriting, public sale, or distribution of stocks, bonds, debentures, notes, or other securities.
Amends the Banking Act of 1933 (Glass-Steagall Act) to expand its prohibition against the transaction of banking activities by securities firms.
Document 2: Conditions treatment as an accrual loan on a non-accrual loan's being current, not more than 30 days delinquent on a monthly payment.

Directs the Financial Stability Oversight Council to study how best to prevent the issuance of contradictory guidance to such institutions by federal banking agencies with respect to loan classifications and capital requirements.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to make it the responsibility of the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) and its Board of Directors, using the powers and authorities conferred by this Act, to resolve depository institution holding companies of failed or failing insured depository institutions.

Permits the FDIC to decline to accept appointment for a covered depository institution holding company, when, in its sole discretion, it determines that resolution of the company would be better accomplished under either federal bankruptcy law or state insurance law.
Document 4: Revises the Community Reinvestment Act of 1977 with regard to assessments of regulated financial institutions.

Specifies the community development and other related activities of a regulated financial institution or a U.S. nonbank financial company, including lending and investment in low- and moderate-income and other traditionally underserved neighborhoods.
Document 5: Amends the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 to require an issuer that is a savings and loan holding company to register with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) if its assets exceed $10 million.
Requires termination of such registration after a savings and loan holding company certifies that its holders of record of that class of security have been reduced to fewer than 1,200 persons.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to include a covered bond as a qualified financial contract with an insured depository institution.
Defines such a bond as a nondeposit recourse debt obligation of an insured depository institution, with a term to maturity of at least one year, which is secured by specifically identified assets.
Document 7: Amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act to authorize the Financial Stability Oversight Council (FSOC) to subject a bank holding company to enhanced supervision and prudential standards.
FSOC's determination must be based upon specified factors, using an indicator-based measurement approach established by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision to determine systemic importance.

topic.name: Wildlife Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative measures aimed at the conservation and management of endangered species and wildlife populations in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973 to direct the Secretary of the Interior in counting the number of a species.
Determining whether a species is endangered or threatened based on comprehensive species counts.
Document 2: Requires Administrators to include estimates of compliance costs for conservation in billing statements.
Provides annual reports on costs related to environmental compliance affecting fish and wildlife.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of the Interior to use empirical data for species listing decisions.
Mandates public availability of information related to species determinations.
Document 4: Amends the Migratory Bird Treaty Act to allow baiting under specific conditions for agricultural producers.
Document 5: Grants the Secretary of the Interior authority to authorize electronic duck stamps.
Amends wildlife restoration acts to support public target range development.
Document 6: Requires the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service to publish a revised recovery plan for the Mexican gray wolf populations.
Establishes a process for state wildlife authorities to manage wolf populations.
Document 7: Allows states to take management actions to stem the decline of Shiras Moose populations.
Mandates data collection and analysis for wildlife management efforts.

topic.name: Government Legislation and Designations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts and designations related to government facilities and financial regulations.

Document 1: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 1900 West Gray Street in Houston, Texas, as the 'Hazel Hainsworth Young Post Office Building.'
Document 2: Local Courthouse Safety Act of 2012 - Amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 to permit the Director of the Department of Justice's (DOJ) Bureau of Justice Assistance to carry out a training and technical assistance program to teach employees of state, local, and tribal law enforcement agencies how to anticipate, survive, and respond to violent encounters.
Document 3: Bureau of Consumer Financial Protection-Inspector General Reform Act of 2013 or CFPB-IG Act of 2013 - Amends the Inspector General Act of 1978 to repeal the authority of the Chairman of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System (Board) to appoint the Inspector General of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB).
Document 4: Eliminate Privacy Notice Confusion Act - Amends the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act to exempt from its annual privacy policy notice requirement any financial institution which provides nonpublic personal information only in accordance with specified requirements.
Document 5: Small Business Investment Company Capital Act of 2015 - Amends the Small Business Investment Act of 1958 to increase from $225 million to $350 million the maximum amount of outstanding leverage.
Document 6: SECURE Water Amendments Act of 2014 - Amends the Omnibus Public Land Management Act of 2009 to include 'planning for or addressing the impact of drought' among the activities for which the Secretary may make grants.
Document 7: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 1700 Cleveland Avenue in Kansas City, Missouri, as the 'Reverend Earl Abel Post Office Building.'

topic.name: Rail Transportation Safety and Infrastructure
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving safety, security, and infrastructure for rail transportation systems in the United States.

Document 1: Designates the Northeast Corridor as a high-speed rail corridor eligible for grants for high-speed rail capital projects.
Document 2: establish a separate Hazardous Liquids Rail Spill Liability Account to support actions related to preventing and responding to discharges of oil or hazardous substances that result from rail shipments.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to establish a task force to complete a risk assessment of freight and passenger rail transportation.
Document 4: Requires each rail carrier to develop and submit for Secretary of Transportation approval a plan to implement a positive train control system that prevents rail collisions.
Document 5: requires each railroad carrier to submit to the Department of Transportation a list identifying each curve, bridge, tunnel, or other location where the maximum authorized operating speed is at least 20 miles per hour less than the maximum approach speed.
Document 6: allows a tax credit for 25% of the cost of new qualified freight rail infrastructure property and new qualified locomotive property.
Document 7: amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a tax credit for 25% of the cost of new qualified freight rail infrastructure property and qualified locomotive property.

topic.name: U.S. Science and Technology Policy
topic.description: The documents collectively address various aspects of U.S. legislation aimed at improving science, technology, and innovation through federal agencies and initiatives.

Document 1: amends the Nuclear Waste Policy Act of 1982
authorize the Department of Energy (DOE) to enter into new contracts
Document 2: amends the National Institute of Standards and Technology Act
authorize the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) to serve as the President's principal advisor
Document 3: Requires the Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy (OSTP) to establish a body

Document 4: requires the National Science Foundation (NSF) to maintain the intellectual merit and broader impacts criteria
NIST shall research information systems for future cybersecurity needs
Document 5: revises the 21st Century Nanotechnology Research and Development Act
requires agencies participating in the Program to support the activities of committees involved in the setting of standards for nanotechnology
Document 6: direct the Secretary of Energy to conduct research to lower the cost of nuclear reactor systems
develop and publish on the Department of Energy (DOE) website a schedule outlining a five-year strategy
Document 7: requires the Director of the National Science Foundation (NSF) to carry out a program of awarding grants
to ensure that the recipients are from a variety of institutions of higher education

topic.name: Health Care and Pension Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislation focused on health care coverage requirements and pension protections for various groups, including cancer patients, retired coal miners, and multiemployer pension plans.

Document 1: require a group or individual health plan that provides medical and surgical benefits to ensure that inpatient coverage and radiation therapy are provided for breast cancer treatment
Such a plan must provide notice to each participant and beneficiary regarding the coverage required under this bill.
